12.text: That we must not by cursing or by false swearing, nor yet by unnecessary oaths, profane or abuse the name of God; nor by silence or connivance be partakers of these horrible sins in others; and, brief

The third commandment requires the holy and reverend use of God's names, titles, attributes, ordinances, the Word, sacraments, prayer, oaths, vows, lots, his works, and whatsoever else there is whereb

16.text: The ninth commandment forbiddeth whatsoever is prejudicial to truth, or injurious to our own, or our neighbour's, good name.
Yes, truly; for no sin is greater or more provoking to God than the profaning of his name; and therefore he has commanded it to be punished with death.

3.text: The fourth commandment requireth the keeping holy to God such set times as he hath appointed in his word; expressly one whole day in seven, to be a holy Sabbath to himself.

35.text: The eighth commandment requireth the lawful procuring and furthering the wealth and outward estate of ourselves and others.
